programmes.show_description: Some Cupid Kills: Frank and Lu investigate a suspicious death among a team of actors who offer Shakespeare for hire for any occasion.
Commitments: Sitcom set in Chigwell, Essex - a mine of comedy! Sharon's husband wants to dump her for another woman and take the cafe away from her.
An Appetite for Murder: Part One: When a popular but tempestuous celebrity chef is murdered during his cookbook launch, there's no shortage of suspects for Judith, Suzie and Becks.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Wipe That Smile off Your Tape: Laughs with the two sisters and their oversexed neighbour. When Sharon joins a video-dating agency, Tracy decides to try her hand at matchmaking.
In the Firing Line: Smith goes undercover as a contract killer in a drug-dealing feud, but his target is a little too close for comfort.
New. The Appointed Hour: Ruth lies to Davey about her night out with Michelle and Melody, who get her drunk and leave her to sleep on the sofa.
New. Rebirth Day: Julia offers support when an old friend decides to confront her past and Michelle tries to convince Ruth she needs to leave Davey for her own safety.
Under the Thumb: Diane discovers that Jack's father is taking her to court to gain custody, and Tricia attempts to matchmake Chrissie and Carlos, with disastrous results.
All the Time in the World: Jordan gets behind the wheel of a car, with fatal consequences. New Year begins terribly for Ruth when she loses a patient and ruins a blossoming friendship.
In the Firing Line: Smith goes undercover as a contract killer in a drug-dealing feud, but his target is a little too close for comfort.
New. While Christian struggles to help Amira seduce Syed, Ronnie takes over R&R. Pat and Peggy compete for Walford's attention, and Zsa Zsa sets Fat Boy on the road to seduction.
New. Syed tells Christian that he loves Amira, and Ben is targeted by bullies on his way home from football. Ronnie struggles to get through to Jack.
Here and There: Laughter and livestock in the immortal series. James is intrigued by Basil, who works as a cow hand, while Siegfried is disturbed by Mr Dawson.
Charles and Gerald argue over each other's relationship with Laura, and Ken realises James has tricked him into signing a loan agreement.
And Rarest Parts: Frank and Lu investigate a case of paranormal activity when they are approached by a wealthy member of the local trainspotting community.
Parting: Time for a cuppa with Chigwell's most famous sisters. Sharon is faced with an impossible choice when her husband, Chris, tells her he is seriously ill.
Who's That Dancing with Nora Batty, Then?: From the early days when Michael Bates was one of the trio, more antics with the three old men. Nora's chum is emigrating to Australia.
The Changing Face of Rural Blamire: A very early episode of the show that's a national institution. The trio embark upon a disastrous business venture.
Some Enchanted Evening: Battleaxes, old boys and beautiful countryside - the perfect humorous blend. Compo finally gets a chance to seduce Nora Batty with his impeccable manners.
New. Sparring with Death: First Chapter: When Mattie investigates a murder at Wildemarsh Wellness Retreat, she finds several people with distinctly unhealthy intentions towards each other.
The Kembleford Dragon: The sleuth must stop a much-loved institution from going off the rails when the local stationmaster is murdered. Mark Williams stars.
Big Topped: James Bolam and Amanda Redman star in the popular series featuring an eccentric group of ex-police officers. The team investigate the death of a circus ringmaster.
Natural Causes: A gruesome case for Sharon Small as the feisty detective, with Liza Tarbuck as guest star. Havers is teamed up with DI Knight to investigate a drowning in Kent.
And Rarest Parts: Frank and Lu investigate a case of paranormal activity when they are approached by a wealthy member of the local trainspotting community.
Wipe That Smile off Your Tape: Laughs with the two sisters and their oversexed neighbour. When Sharon joins a video-dating agency, Tracy decides to try her hand at matchmaking.
An Appetite for Murder: Part Two: As police investigate the chef's death, a new development makes them rethink everything they have learned so far.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Caring: Comedy with the Essex sisters and their oversexed neighbour. When Sharon and Tracey's aunt comes to convalesce, the sisters discover she can be very demanding.
Thug on the Tyne: Part 1: Frank Burnside goes away from the manor as he asks for Rawton's help in a major undercover operation in Newcastle.
New. Creature Comforts: Ruth accepts Michelle's offer of help and decides to leave Davey. Nick treats a distressed patient.
New. Pass the Parcel: George clashes with a child's mother who is refusing to allow her toddler to have the MMR vaccine, and Ruth returns home to find Davey has left her.
Hard Lesson to Learn: Jess fights to save her brother's pregnant girlfriend, who has gone into premature labour, while Mickie demonstrates surprising surgical skills. With Lucy Speed.
Epiphany: A tense confrontation between Adam and Warren has brutal consequences for Kirsty, while Jordan is kidnapped at knife point and forced to treat the victim of a stabbing.
Thug on the Tyne: Part 1: Frank Burnside goes away from the manor as he asks for Rawton's help in a major undercover operation in Newcastle.
New. Syed and Christian's secret spreads wider when someone stumbles on a tryst. Meanwhile, Ronnie gets a visit from Jack's charming doctor.
New. While Syed wonders if Masood saw him with Christian, a terrified Ben finds other ways to get away from his bullies, and Ronnie is chased by a handsome doctor.
The Course of True Love: Vets, pets and country dialects in the ageless series. The practice is saddened by the death of King George VI, and Siegfried is determined to heal a rift.
Ken is attacked by James, then challenged to a powerboat race by Leo. Meanwhile, Abby is involved in a terrible road accident.
Time Decays: An idyllic Shakespeare walk soon turns to disaster when one of the walkers is murdered and Frank becomes the prime suspect.
Jobs for the Girls: Chigwell comedy with the Essex girls. Sharon and Tracey want spending money for Christmas, so they take up some extra work to pay for last minute presents.
A Quiet Drink: Classic laughs with the boys who are no strangers to getting ushered out of the cafe with the aid of a broom. Someone isn't getting his round in at the local pub.
Ballad for Wind Instruments and Canoe: A trip across Yorkshire with the game old codgers. The trio go on a voyage in a canoe, but it looks like they're upstream without a paddle.
Northern Flying Circus: More madcap adventures with the misbehaving veterans. Compo gets on his bike - motorbike, that is - and tries to out-perform Evel Knievel.
The Angel of Mercy: Mrs McCarthy's friend is the latest villager to die in her sleep, and Father Brown soon senses something sinister is afoot. Fifties-set drama, starring Mark Williams.
The gritty police drama series about a Yorkshire police sergeant continues. Catherine sinks into a deep depression as she learns that Tommy is still at large.
Spare Parts: Popular series about a team of ex-cops investigating unsolved cases. Here, they probe the murder of a wealthy businessman, and uncover an organ donor racket.
One Guilty Deed: Another case for the British detective duo who sprang from the mind of a top US crime writer. Lynley and Havers find a sinister underworld in a seaside town.
